concerns.text: The reconstruction step falls back to using the average of the two variant rates when a baseline conversion rate is absent (`abs_diff = lift_percent / 100 * baseline_rate` or *average of the two variant rates*). This assumption can bias the reconstructed effect size and p‑value, threatening construct validity of the inconsistency check.

Thresholds are described as “configurable constants in `config.py`” (0.05 for p‑values, 5 % for effect size and sample size) but the plan provides no empirical justification or sensitivity analysis of these cut‑offs beyond a placeholder “[deferred]”. Without concrete evidence the chosen tolerances may be arbitrary.

The pipeline relies on a synthetic validation set of 200 entries for FR‑004a sensitivity analysis. Synthetic data may not capture the full distribution of real‑world reporting quirks, so performance metrics (false‑positive/false‑negative rates) could be overly optimistic.

The mixed‑effects logistic regression includes random intercepts for `source` and `month` only. No random slopes or additional covariates (e.g., industry, test type) are modelled, which may leave residual confounding unaddressed.

The plan states that multiple‑comparison correction is “not required beyond the explicit tests listed”, yet many source‑wise and temporal tests are performed. Without a formal correction (e.g., Bonferroni, FDR) the overall Type I error rate may be inflated.

The design does not consider the possibility that multiple URLs may refer to the same underlying experiment, leading to double‑counting in aggregate statistics and biasing the inconsistency proportion.
